items.summary: Leaders at Finra, the brokerage industry’s self-regulator, are piloting a program to encourage firms to self-report compliance failures.

items.title: At 100, the Bajaj Group is writing a new chapter—on healthcare
items.summary: After 100 years of saying no to diversification, one of India’s fastest-growing wealth engines is finally scratching its ultimate itch. Powered by a ₹5,800-crore dividend chest, the billionaire cousins from the Bajaj Group have said ‘yes’ to one sector: healthcare.

items.title: Taiwan was front and center during Trump's China trip — but absent from Xi’s meeting with Putin
items.summary: The meetings between China's Xi Jinping and his U.S. and Russian counterparts came just days apart, but there was a stark contrast in tone and content.

items.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures rise up to 0.8% as crude oil prices ease; Nvidia earnings in focus
items.summary: US stock futures showed a slight positive trend on May 20, after three sessions of losses driven by rising bond yields and stalled US-Iran negotiations. Nasdaq 100 futures rose 0.8%, while S&P 500 and Dow futures gained 0.4% and 0.2% respectively.

items.title: Edible oil industry body calls for boosting output, adopting modern farming amid West Asia crisis
items.summary: India's edible oil imports are a significant economic concern. The Solvent Extractors' Association of India urges increased domestic oilseed production and smarter consumption. These steps are vital for national economic and strategic resilience. The industry seeks policy support to navigate global disruptions and currency fluctuations. Focusing on self-sufficiency offers a path to avoid future crises.

items.title: Fuel duty freeze extended until the end of the year
items.summary: Fuel duty was initially cut by 5p in March 2022, under the Conservative government.

items.title: Milma to raise milk price by Rs 4 per litre from June 1
items.summary: Milma has announced a Rs 4 per litre milk price increase in Kerala, effective June 1. The cooperative's Board of Directors decided to pass on the majority of this hike to dairy farmers, with 83.75% of the increase benefiting them. This revision, previously approved by the government, was delayed due to election code of conduct.

items.title: UN agency flags India growth slowdown to 6.4% in FY27 as Iran war fuels global inflation, cost pressures
items.summary: A UN report has warned of weaker global growth, rising inflation and tighter financial conditions, even as India remains among the fastest-growing major economies with a gradual recovery expected in FY28.

items.title: Kalpataru, Shangrila Infracon plan debt fundraise via private credit funds: Report
items.summary: They are likely to receive interest from private credit funds, which have ​been on the lookout for decent-sized deals, one of the sources said.

India bonds gain on foreign-bank buying as oil cools
items.summary: The benchmark 6.48% 2035 bond yield dipped 3.4 basis points to end at 7.0761%, easing ‌the most in ⁠two weeks. ⁠Bond yields move inversely to prices.

items.title: India's toffee exports jump 166% in 12 years to reach Rs 132 crore in 2025-26
items.summary: India's toffee exports have seen a remarkable surge. They climbed 166 percent over 12 years, reaching Rs 132 crore in 2025-26. This growth was highlighted by Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal. The announcement followed a warm exchange between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni. Meloni thanked Modi for gifting her 'Melody' toffees.

items.title: Jubilant FoodWorks Q4 Results: Domino's parent net profit jumps 67% to Rs 82 crore
items.summary: Its consolidated revenue from operations in the fourth quarter was Rs 2,499.46 crore against Rs 2,095.02 crore in the year-ago period, it added.

items.title: Karnataka HC stays FSSAI advisory on use of Ashwagandha in major relief to US-based Kerry Inc, 7 other firms
items.summary: In a significant decision, the Karnataka High Court has granted temporary relief to eight businesses, including Kerry Inc, putting a hold on a government advisory that limited the use of Ashwagandha leaves in dietary and health products. Previously, the Food Safety and Standards Authority of India had barred these leaves and their extracts.

items.title: UK waters down new Russian oil sanctions as fuel prices rise
items.summary: The change reflects increasing supply concerns over certain fuels due to the effective blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: College career path ‘over’ as skilled trade get 30% pay bump, recruitment giant says
items.summary: "I would say you can make a good career and good money in skilled trade. That's definitely a career track," CEO of world's largest recruitment firm told CNBC.

items.title: Parle Melody goes stock out as PM Modi gifts the toffee to Italian counterpart Meloni
items.summary: An unrelated stock with the Parle name in the real estate segment hit a 5% upper circuit thanks to the news on Wednesday.

items.title: Nifty erases gap-down, closes higher as energy stocks lead recovery
items.summary: The BSE Sensex settled at 75,318.39, up 117.54 points or 0.16%, while the Nifty 50 closed at 23,659, gaining 41 points or 0.17%

items.summary: Jakarta to set up state-owned enterprise that would be sole exporter of palm oil, thermal coal and nickel

items.title: India's infrastructure output grows 1.7% in April; cement, steel, electricity lift core sectors
items.summary: India's core infrastructure output saw a 1.7% growth in April 2026. Cement, steel, and electricity production expanded strongly. However, coal, crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, and fertilisers experienced contractions. For the full fiscal year 2025-26, cumulative growth reached 2.7%.

items.title: Gainers & Losers: BEL,Zee among 6topstockmovers on Wednesday
items.summary: ​Domestic markets closed marginally higher, led by auto and bank stocks. Deccan Gold Mines surged 11% on a mineral discovery, and GE Vernova gained 7% after strong Q4 performance.​

items.title: Sell 100 grams of gold to help India; Feroze Azeez makes the case
items.summary: Feroze Azeez urges Indian households to sell 2-4% of their idle gold, valued at $4 trillion, to curb excessive imports. He frames this as financial patriotism, highlighting that selling gold near historic peaks is smart profit booking. This initiative aims to reduce the $75 billion annual import bill and bolster the rupee. The govt must facilitate this by temporarily removing capital gain tax on such sales.

items.title: Receiving Bharat Tex queries despite global disruptions: Textiles Secretary
items.summary: Bharat Tex 2026 is making news, with the Textile Ministry noting a surge in interest from international exhibitors and diplomatic representatives. Engagement remains robust despite ongoing global challenges and anticipation is on the rise, the ministry says.

items.title: Q4 Results Today Live: Ola Electric & Grasim narrows loss in Q4, Lenskart & Jubilant Foodworks profit decline, Samvardhana Motherson con. profit up, Honeywell Automation, Apollo, Bosch, Medplus to announce Q4 results
items.summary: Q4 Results Today Live Updates: Stay tuned to businessline for the latest live updates on Q4 earnings.

items.title: India needs to raise R&D spending to 2 pc of GDP by 2035 to boost manufacturing: Report
items.summary: India's manufacturing growth is hampered by low R&D spending, currently at 0.6% of GDP, a report highlights. To boost its manufacturing share, India aims to increase R&D expenditure to 2% by 2035, requiring greater private sector involvement and stronger innovation ecosystems. This transition to innovation-driven manufacturing is crucial for long-term global competitiveness.

items.title: FIIs won't return to Indian markets in a hurry; only 3 triggers may bring them back: Amar K Ambani
items.summary: Foreign institutional investors (FIIs) are unlikely to return to Indian equities soon due to structural and cyclical forces, including modest dollar returns and the AI revolution favoring other markets. FIIs may only re-enter if valuations hit rock bottom, IPO activity surges, or global markets become overheated, making India an attractive diversification option.

items.title: SpaceX picks Goldman Sachs for lead left position on record-breaking IPO, sources say
items.summary: SpaceX has picked Goldman Sachs to lead what's expected to be a record-setting IPO.

items.title: Huge potential to boost food, beverages exports to UAE: TPCI
items.summary: India’s food and beverage exports to the UAE are poised for major growth, with current shipments valued at USD 3.6 billion compared to the UAE’s total imports of USD 22 billion. Holding a 10 percent market share, India is emerging as a trusted supplier of quality food products to the Gulf nation. Industry experts believe the India-UAE free trade agreement and the upcoming Indo-Food Park in Mumbai will further strengthen trade ties, enhance export infrastructure, and unlock new opportunities for Indian exporters in the UAE market.

items.title: Trump makes over 3,700 trades in Q1; Nvidia, Intel, Microsoft, Netflix part of portfolio
European stocks rise: UK inflation and elevated bond yields in focus
items.summary: European stocks rose on Wednesday as global markets keep a close eye on elevated bond yields and UK inflation data.

items.title: The ticking price tag: What delaying parenthood really costs you financially and biologically
items.summary: A growing number of couples are choosing to delay parenthood in pursuit of career milestones, financial stability, and personal goals. But what happens when the biological clock and the bank account collide?

items.title: AI helping India's engineering hubs generate IP faster, Daimler Truck executive says
items.summary: Indian engineering hubs for global firms are accelerating intellectual property generation and mitigating supply chain risks, according to a Daimler Truck executive. AI is further speeding up innovation and engineering work, as companies increasingly rely on local hubs for product development. This trend is also influencing hiring, with a growing demand for niche talents in AI and cybersecurity.

items.title: Crude-linked energy crisis, softer monsoon may slow FMCG growth in 2026: Report
items.summary: Indian FMCG volume growth is expected to slow down this year. Geopolitical tensions and a potential below-normal monsoon are key concerns. Companies may implement pricing strategies, impacting consumption frequency. In a base scenario, growth could reach 5 percent. However, adverse conditions might push growth to 3-4 percent. Consumers are consolidating purchases, buying less often but more per trip.

items.title: Swiss finance faces some awkward questions
items.summary: Activists have triggered a vote on becoming a ‘more sustainable financial centre’

items.title: Inside India’s biggest banking and financial services tech hubs
items.summary: Standard Chartered plans to cut over 7,000 jobs in four years, replacing human capital with technology, particularly impacting back-office roles in India. This move aligns with a broader trend of financial firms leveraging AI and tech hubs in India for both support and higher-value functions, though some, like Wells Fargo, are also consolidating operations.

items.title: Harikanta Overseas IPO Day 1: Check subscription, GMP, offer price and other details
items.summary: Harikanta Overseas IPO price band has been fixed at ₹91 to ₹96 per share, with the company looking to raise ₹35.6 crore at the upper end of the offer price.

items.title: Alembic Pharma bets on branded drugs in the US
items.summary: While the company hopes to push into branded speciality drugs, which offer a better value proposition in the US, its core generics business will continue to remain a priority

items.title: Banks need to push Atal Pension Yojana subscribers toward higher pension slab: DFS Secy
items.summary: Financial institutions are urged to guide Atal Pension Yojana subscribers towards higher pension slabs. Many subscribers remain in the minimum Rs 1,000 monthly pension category. This amount may not be sufficient in the future. Banks must counsel individuals to choose plans reflecting future needs and current affordability. Efforts are also underway to re-examine the scheme for potentially higher pension payouts.

items.title: NCDEX launches India’s first weather derivatives contract based on Mumbai rainfall
items.summary: NCDEX has announced India’s first SEBI-approved exchange-traded weather derivatives contract, “RAINMUMBAI”, aimed at helping market participants hedge risks arising from monsoon rainfall fluctuations. The contract, based on IMD rainfall data and developed with IIT Bombay, seeks to create a regulated framework for managing climate-linked financial risks across sectors.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Stock markets rebound; Sensex climbs 117 points on fag-end buying
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Highlights: Benchmark equity indices rebounded on Wednesday on fag-end buying and a sharp rally in blue-chip Reliance Industries.

items.title: BLS International posts net profit of Rs 724 crore in FY26
items.summary: BLS International achieved its highest-ever performance in financial year 2026. Revenues surged to Rs 2998 crore, a 36.7% year-on-year increase. Net profit rose 34% to Rs 724 crore. The company's visa and consular services segment saw significant margin expansion. Several new contracts were secured during the year, contributing to this robust financial outcome.

items.title: BOC Aviation to lease three more Boeing 737-8200 aircraft to Akasa Air
items.summary: BOC Aviation has signed a new deal to lease three Boeing 737-8200 aircraft to Akasa Air. These planes will be delivered by the end of 2026. This is the second such agreement between the two companies. Akasa Air is expanding its fleet with these fuel-efficient aircraft. Indian airlines are increasing capacity to meet growing passenger demand.

items.title: Central Bank of India to open 150 branches, recruit about 1,400 officers in FY27: MD
items.summary: Central Bank of India is planning a major nationwide expansion with the opening of 150 new branches across India. To support its growth strategy, the public sector lender will recruit around 1,400 employees, including new officers, while also strengthening operations across its subsidiaries. The move is aimed at enhancing customer outreach, improving banking services, and accelerating business growth in key markets.

items.title: Inflation falls to 2.8% but is expected to rise from here
items.summary: Energy prices were lower due to the government's energy bill support package and lower wholesale prices before the Iran war.

items.title: BPCL taps US LPG spot market for first time amid Gulf supply disruption
items.summary: BPCL has imported LPG cargoes from the US for the first time as India diversifies energy supplies amid West Asia conflict and Strait of Hormuz disruptions. The move aims to reduce dependence on the region, which earlier supplied nearly 90% of India’s LPG imports.

items.title: Rupee hits record low near 97/USD on oil, US Treasury yield strain
items.summary: The rupee slid to 96.96 per dollar, breaching its previous record low of 96.6150 hit in the prior session, and ended ‌the day at 96.82

items.title: Ola Electric Q4 Results: From narrowing net loss to margin outlook - Key takeaways from EV maker’s earnings
items.summary: Ola Electric's Q4 loss narrowed to ₹500 crore, a 42.5% decrease year-on-year. Revenue dropped 57% to ₹265 crore, while gross margin improved to 38.5%. The company’s shares rose by nearly 1% to ₹36.94.

items.title: Miniratna defence PSU Bharat Dynamics sets board meeting date to declare Q4 results, final dividend for FY26
items.summary: The defence stock said its board will meet on Thursday, 28 May, to consider and approve the audited financial results for the quarter and year ended 31 March 2026. Bharat Dynamics added that its board will also consider payout of a final dividend at its meeting next week.

items.title: Global investors in wait-and-watch mode, but India is no longer optional: DWS, Nippon Life AMC

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on May 20: Siemens, Tata Comm, Hindalco, HPCL, Paytm, Bajaj Auto among top gainers
items.summary: Indian stock markets remained range-bound for the fourth session, with Nifty 50 gaining 0.17% and BSE Sensex up 0.11%. The rupee fell to a record low of 96.96 against the dollar amid high oil prices and US-Iran tensions, impacting investor sentiment.

items.title: Stock market rebounds: Sensex recovers 790 points from day’s low, Nifty closes above 23,650
items.summary: Indian stock markets staged a strong recovery, erasing morning losses. The Sensex and Nifty closed higher as oil prices dropped below $110 per barrel. Bond yields also eased from record highs. Investor sentiment improved broadly across market segments. The Indian Rupee, however, hit a fresh record low against the US dollar. Foreign investors resumed selling Indian equities.

items.title: Only 8% of Atal Pension Yojana’s subscribers opt for ₹5,000 monthly slab; 87% choose minimum sum of ₹1,000
items.summary: The Atal Pension Yojana, launched in 2015, has over 90 million enrolments, with significant participation from younger age groups. The persistency rate stood at 50.1% as of 31 March.

items.title: Higher global prices, tighter imports to boost India’s oilseed production: SEA
items.summary: Domestic edible oil prices are moving in tandem with imported oil prices, thereby supporting mustard prices, says association chief Sanjeev Asthana

items.title: India needs $10-15 billion worth land parcels in five years to set up solar, wind energy projects: Colliers
items.summary: India needs substantial land for renewable energy expansion. By 2030, approximately 7 lakh acres will be required for solar and wind projects. This land acquisition is estimated to cost between USD 10 to 15 billion. The country is set to significantly increase its renewable energy capacity, driving investment and development across various sectors.

items.title: ‘A decisive phase’: Giorgia Meloni says India-Italy ties are being rewritten at speed
items.summary: India and Italy are entering a significant new era in their relationship. Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni met with Prime Minister Narendra Modi. Their nations are now forging a special strategic partnership. This partnership is built on shared values of freedom and democracy. It reflects a common outlook for the future. Ties have grown with remarkable speed.

items.title: Ola Electric Q4 Results: Net loss contracts 42% YoY to Rs 500 crore, revenue tanks 57%
items.summary: Ola Electric Q4 Results: Pure-play electric two-wheeler maker Ola Electric Mobility reported a consolidated net loss of Rs 500 crore for the March quarter, marking a contraction of 42.5% from Rs 870 crore reported in the same period last year. This is attributable to the owners of the company.

items.title: Air India group active fleet shrinks while rivals expand summer capacity
items.summary: Air India is flying up to 5% fewer domestic flights and nearly 40% fewer international routes this summer versus a year ago, squeezed by a depleted fleet, soaring jet fuel costs, and wartime airspace closures.

items.title: Stock market today: Sensex gains over 100 points, Nifty 50 ends at 23,659
items.summary: Stock market today: The Sensex ended with a gain of 118 points, or 0.16%, at 75,318.39, while the Nifty 50 settled at 23,659, up 41 points, or 0.17%.

items.title: Lenskart Q4 Results: Profit falls 9% YoY to Rs 200 crore, but revenue jumps 46%
items.summary: Lenskart Solutions saw its net profit decrease by 9% year-on-year in the fourth quarter. However, revenue from operations surged by 46%. The company conducted significantly more eye tests, expanding its market reach. International business also showed robust growth. Lenskart is focusing on premiumization and improving capital efficiency, funding its expansion internally.

items.title: A strange paradox has taken hold of the global fashion industry
items.summary: Fashion brands are doubling down on sustainability even as value-seeking shoppers ignore the pitch.

items.title: No relief in memory prices visible over next 12-18 months, says IESA president
items.summary: Memory prices are set to rise for consumers. No major new production capacity is expected in the next 18 months. This will lead to higher costs for devices like smartphones. The ongoing West Asia crisis also impacts raw material availability for electronics. India Semiconductor Mission 2.0 aims to boost the local chip ecosystem and reduce import reliance.

items.title: These 14 smallcap stocks rally up to 250% in just 6 months; 4 turn multibaggers
items.summary: Despite a sharp market correction and Sensex decline, several Indian smallcap stocks delivered exceptional six-month returns, with select counters surging up to 259% and multiple stocks emerging as multibagger wealth creators.

items.title: BHIM Payments App records 300% surge in FY26 transactions
items.summary: BHIM Payments App saw a significant 301% surge in transaction volumes during FY26. More users embraced the platform for daily purchases like groceries and dining. West Bengal showed strong growth, with peer-to-merchant payments dominating. The app's accessibility and new features are driving this increased adoption across India.

items.title: Samvardhana Motherson Q4 results: Cons profit jumps 46% YoY to Rs 1,497 crore, revenue rises 8%
items.summary: Samvardhana Motherson International reported a 46% year-on-year rise in Q4FY26 consolidated net profit to Rs 1,497 crore, driven by steady business growth. Revenue from operations increased 8% to Rs 32,356 crore during the quarter.

items.title: TV presenter says abusive ex gave her no access to her own money
items.summary: Ruth Dodsworth's former husband was jailed for coercive and controlling behaviour and stalking.

items.title: Waiting for your dream phone to get cheaper? That moment may never come
items.summary: Smartphone prices are no longer dropping predictably. The demand for AI chips is causing component costs to surge. This means phones are launching at higher prices and staying that way. Budget phones are seeing downgrades to control costs. Consumers may need to buy when they need a phone, as big discounts are fading.

items.title: ‘Future of Standard Chartered depends on talent’: CEO tells staff after ‘lower-value human capital’ comment backfires
items.summary: Bill Winters, CEO of Standard Chartered, addressed staff regarding concerns over automation and AI replacing jobs. He emphasized the bank's commitment to workforce evolution and the importance of talent for its future.

items.title: Expert View | Why are gold, silver rates likely to remain volatile in 2026? Kaynat Chainwala of Kotak Securities answers
items.summary: India’s external balance remains far more sensitive to crude oil prices than to bullion imports. So, gold and silver can amplify the pressure, but crude is still the main swing factor as energy imports remain the dominant driver of dollar outflows, says Kaynat Chainwala of Kotak Securities.

items.title: Cement companies' profitability to moderate in FY27 on rising fuel, logistics costs: ICRA
items.summary: In FY27, Indian cement manufacturers are anticipated to experience a notable dip in profits, ranging from 10-15%. The escalating prices of power and fuel, fueled by geopolitical tensions in West Asia, are set to exert a financial pinch. Although cement prices are likely to rise, they will not fully bridge the gap created by increasing input costs.

items.title: Gold loans jump over 50% y-o-y, drive India's retail credit growth: Report
items.summary: Report says total retail loans outstanding reached ₹170.2 lakh crore as of March 2026, registering a 16.6% year-on-year growth and a 4.6% quarter-on-quarter increase

items.title: Three supertankers attempt Hormuz crossing
items.summary: Vessels bound for China and South Korea are carrying 6mn barrels of crude oil

items.title: Glottis stock surges 20% on strong volumes, logs biggest one-day jump in more than a month
items.summary: Glottis shares rose 20% on May 20, reaching ₹72.30, marking a six-month high. Despite no major triggers, trading volume spiked to 66 million shares. The company will announce its March quarter results on May 25, after reporting a decline in revenue and profit for Q3FY26.
